F Foundry Theme authoring
Home Overview Plugins Architecture Themes
Authoring Guide

Building a theme

Foundry themes own presentation. A theme defines layouts, partials, theme assets, and a small manifest that describes the package.

Start with the scaffold

foundry theme scaffold editorial
foundry theme validate editorial
foundry theme switch editorial

The scaffolded theme gives you the expected directory structure and the required base set of layouts and partials.

Theme structure

themes/editorial/
  theme.yaml
  assets/
    css/
      base.css
  layouts/
    base.html
    index.html
    page.html
    post.html
    list.html
    partials/
      head.html
      header.html
      footer.html

Manifest fields

theme.yaml describes the theme and lists the layouts and slots it expects to support.

name: editorial
title: Editorial
version: 0.1.0
description: A Foundry theme.
license: MIT
min_foundry_version: 0.1.0
layouts:
  - base
  - index
  - page
  - post
  - list

Layout responsibilities

  • base.html: wraps the page shell and usually includes shared head, header, footer, and plugin slots.
  • index.html: renders the home page or language root page.
  • page.html: renders page documents.
  • post.html: renders post documents.
  • list.html: renders list-style views such as taxonomy archives.

Theme and plugin interplay

Theme templates should leave room for plugin-provided markup. The scaffold includes slot calls such as head.end, body.start, body.end, and post/page content-adjacent slots.

Assets

Put theme CSS and other theme-owned assets under assets/. During build or preview, Foundry copies theme assets into public/theme/ and bundles discovered CSS into the main CSS output.

Validation checklist

  1. Run foundry theme validate <name>.
  2. Preview with foundry serve or foundry serve-preview.
  3. Confirm page, post, and taxonomy archive templates all render cleanly.
  4. Check that plugin slots still have sensible placement.

Reference theme

The default theme in the repository is the canonical reference for current layout expectations and slot placement.

Start from the scaffold, then compare against the default theme when you need a concrete reference.
